channel.title: Comments for page "Log Of Anomalous Items"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Log Of Anomalous Items"

item.title: Some edit suggestions
item.content:encoded:
Just going through this article again! I noticed a couple of grammatical mistakes, so I thought I'd suggest them here, along with some other changes. I'm suggesting them here because the article's locked, and I can't make them myself. I am by no means a professional, so I might not always be accurate.

+ Minor editing suggestions
- This is way longer than I thought it'd be

with David Byrne repeating the line and looking increasingly distraught as hands grip his head.

This line should probably say his hands grip his head, unless the author wanted to imply that there were several disembodied hands present.

Current Status: … Well, heh, looks like… w-was dad really like that? … I need to go lie down… *wimper*

"wimper" should be spelled Whimper.

A Carl Thompson Rainbow Bass. When any song of the band Primus is played, a voice is heard saying "Primus Sucks!"

There should probably be an "on it" after the played.

Item Description: A piece of paper. Descriptions of the item will transform the texts medium into paper.

"Texts" should be spelled with an apostrophe.

that will play any song, with vocals in Kurt Donald Cobain's voice.

The comma there is unnecessary.

Despite the soft appearance,

The "the" should be an its.

will indicate that illegal contraband exist within its conpartments,

The "exist" should be plural.

As much as a small drag of a fingernail along the phone, top to bottom,

This sentence feels very clunky. Removing both commas and inserting "from" before "top to bottom" should help a little.

that show stylized logo of various GoI in

"logo" should be plural.

woman in the painting appear to staring at whoever is loo

Should be "appears" and "be staring"

+ Extra editing suggestions
- Hide extra editing suggestions

Individuals witnessing the resulting effect from this item will feel a strong urge to support the item, usually by compliments.

I feel like the additional "from this item" is unnecessary. The description feels clunkier with that in there. Also, just saying "support" is a little vague for foundation protocol. I think inserting something like "provide the object with emotional support" would make more sense.

A white glass plate seven inches in diameter where anything eaten from it is given a strong taste of salt, even if it did not contain any.

This doesn't read Foundation to me at all. I'd suggest rewording the description to something like this: "A white glass plate seven inches in diameter. Any food eaten off of it tastes strongly of salt, even if none was used in its cooking."

A .55 calibre bullet with the date of manufacture as 8/2/1898. The bullet was carbon dated back to 1898 being the only of it's kind created back then. The bullet can be fired an innumerous amount of times.

Same with this one. I'd try changing it to something like "A .55 calibre bullet. Carbon dating suggests it was manufactured in 1898. As no other ammunition manufactured back then matches this object's type, it is assumed to be one of a kind. The bullet can be fired an infinite amount of times without being spent."

That's all I could find for now! I'm fairly new, so if anything I suggested was unwarranted or incorrect, please let me know! Hope this helps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Item Description: A bronze statue of a mermaid. Causes kleptomaniacal compulsions in mammalian subjects continually exposed to it.

How did this NOT end up stolen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same happened to me.


If anyone's wondering what he's talking about click this.

item.title: Double Colon in One Entry
item.content:encoded:
>Item Description: A generic pink "piggy bank" containing an endless amount of United States Quarter-Dollars.
Location of Recovery: Fairbanks, Alaska
Date of Recovery::1/22/12
Current Status: In Storage
Notice: Any Staff caught using this device to further their wealth will be severely reprimanded. -Dr. Garbers

The date of recovery has multiple colons. Post is locked so I could not make the edit, myself.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The images used on this list were provided by Penton, who confirmed that the images were provided by a friend and that the images are released under CC BY-SA 3.0.

item.title: Ahahahaha
item.content:encoded:
NICE SHOT!!!!
I think I probably did that before, but I was lucky I didn't get stuck…
(God heavens if I really got stuck I may lose a finger?…

+ Something I wanna tell you guys
- Yeah, that's it!

I've heard that people in Northeast China have warned their friends and children not to lick the telegraph poles in winter. (Well, I'm not the local resident so I cannot be sure if this is real) They said if people do this, they may find their tongues stick to that telegraph pole and they cannot make their tongues off!
By the way, I think SCP-026-J has explained that phenomenon… Oh I bursted out laughing when I read that! (Ahahahaha

item.title: Something about this list in the CN branch
item.content:encoded:
Interesting.
In the CN branch we vote for every single item since April…
So if you write a boring one you may receive quite a few downvotes. Oh that's so damn hard for me to have an item in the list! :-D
Well, I wrote one in the CN branch. But it was only +6…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn it that [REDACTED] link got me and it’s 2020

props to who can figure out which item I’m referring to

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. Don't know why

item.title: Crocodile Head
item.content:encoded:
Item Description: A severed saltwater crocodile (Crocodylus porosus) head, which doesn't experience decomposition or corrosion. When touched or handled physically, the head animates and bites its handler, then returns to its inactive state. Object also regenerates almost instantaneously when damaged.
Date of Recovery: ██-██-2014
Location of Recovery: Las Vegas, Nevada
Current Status: In frozen storage

This at all related to 682? Sounds like 682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Description: A wooden board, 1 foot by 2 feet by 1 inch thick, with an approximately finger-sized hole. When a finger is fully inserted into the hole, the hole will invariably shrink such that the finger will become inextricable without considerable effort, no matter the actual size of the finger.
Date of Recovery: ██-██-████
Location of Recovery: ████████, █████
Status: Stored in a standard containment locker in Non-Essential Anomaly Storage, Site-19

item.title: Random item description
item.content:encoded: Somewhere in the list this appears, Beskrivning av föremål:, it's just that nothing else, is it a mistake?

item.title: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: No uncollapsed images on the forums, please.

item.title: Giant Causeway
item.content:encoded:
"Item Description: A 24000-carat diamond, cut in the size and shape of a common construction brick.
Date of Recovery: ██-██-197█
Location of Recovery: ███████, South Africa
Current Status: In storage pending identification of source."

Well..

+ show block
– hide block

How about this one, then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since reading this list, I've been spontaneously saying "Nice shot!" at random throughout the day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aware that this is old but i was compelled to comment,

That's my secret…i always have cravings for cheese…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Item Description: A 76-centimeter-tall statue of a clown. In room where it was placed, a giggling sound would be noted whenever lights were turned off. Current Status: Shot approximately 15 times with a 9mm sidearm by Agent ██████. Agent reprimanded. No anomalous properties recorded in the remains.

…..

Appropriate response, though it should have been shot WAAAY more times.

item.title: Android Charging Cable
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn’t this be a Micro USB cable? In terms of hardware, “Android device” is meaningless.

Alternatively, clarify that this somehow works with any device running an operating system called “Android”, and treat that as an anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are so many typos on this page, it really needs proofreading. I know English may not be everyone’s first language, but some entries here just seem thoughtless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless that solution is a strategy to counter the Foundation’s efforts at recontainment. It could result in XK, so it definitely would be denied. Especially after the incident with the nerfing gun

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"A yellow "rubber ducky" bath toy. When a subject explains in detail a practical problem to the item as though it were a living anthropomorphic duck, they will feel that they have a better understanding of said problem, and are often immediately able to come up with a solution."

Potential 682 killer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: could we please number these? it would be useful for looking them up.

item.title: Re: Small Punctuation Error
item.content:encoded: I just noticed how inconsistent all the dates are… It should be 2012 instead of just 12 like the majority of the items, with 4 digits in the year. I also noticed how some dates use dashes and others slashes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the anomalous items here - the box that makes everything upside down, could be slightly improved with CSS.

Item Description: A box that makes all text that describes it upside down.
Date of Recovery: ████-██-██
Location of Recovery: Dr. Bright's office.
Curent Status: In storage
Notes: Can I have it back? - Dr. Bright

 [[div style="transform: scaleY(-1) scaleX(-1)"]] **Item Description:** A box that makes all text that describes it upside down. **Date of Recovery:** ████-██-██ **Location of Recovery:** Dr. Bright's office. **Curent Status:** In storage //Notes: Can I have it back? - Dr. Bright// [[/div]]

There are a couple benefits to doing this: most notably, it's easier to edit and fix typoes (the current version says "dr. bright"), and the letters will all properly flip (there's some weird stuff going on right now - different sizes, weights and alignments on letters.) This version, when flipped upside down, is a 1:1 equivalent for what it should be.

Edit: Right justifying the text in the div will cause it to left-align and look closer to what's currently on the page, if that's desired.

item.title: Small Punctuation Error
item.content:encoded:
This entry has a double colon by the date of recovery. The page is currently locked.

Item Description: A generic pink "piggy bank" containing an endless amount of United States Quarter-Dollars.
Location of Recovery: Fairbanks, Alaska
Date of Recovery::1/22/12
Current Status: In Storage
Notice: Any Staff caught using this device to further their wealth will be severely reprimanded. -Dr. Garbers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [self delete: pls delete]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should it become necessary you could always go with what SCP-913 did and separate all the entries by additional tabs and pages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 I learned about the word apposite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addressed, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is an error.

Item Description: A 4oz cup of ████ brand applesauce, contents nonperishable. Expiration date always matches current date.
Date of Recovery: 6-18-2016
Location of Recovery: Gold Hill, Oregon
Current Status: Inadvertently consumed by Junior Researcher ██████. ██████ suffered mild indigestion but recovered within 12 hours.
Item Description: A goldfish which produces a constant stream of fresh water from its mouth at the rate of 10.9 L/s.
Date of Recovery: 12-10-2016
Location of Recovery: Rhodehampton, New South Wales
Current Status: Forcibly imploded following a blockage in the drainage system for its tank.

There needs to be a space between these two entries but the page is locked for me to do anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that it has been pruned, can staff please capitalize the "Current status: [REDACTED]." in the YouTube USB Flash drive entry. I can't do it because it's locked.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
This first Log of Anomalous Items, grown and groomed over the past nine years, is now locked for further edits and a second log is now open for business.

Thank you to all the wonderful authors over the years to have contributed to the first log, and many thanks to AbsentmindedNihilist, Modern_Erasmus, and Veiedhimaedhr who did the heavy lifting of the final prune, along with longtime crit experts and pruners Zyn, Dexanote, and stormbreath who helped make it happen.

item.title: Another one for GoI: Crossy Clovers
item.content:encoded:
Moved to 2nd thread.

Location of Recovery: A cafe in Kichijoji that seemed to collaborate with "the comic of the end of a century"

item.title: Wafers Draft 1
item.content:encoded:
(Here’s my draft! Feel free to comment!)

Item Description: A bag of ~470 (originally 500) stale communion wafers. If a wafer is consumed by a human sentient individual, the subject will become completely convinced that they are “going to Hell when they die”, regardless of prior religious or spiritual beliefs. Responses to this anomaly vary depending on the subject, but are often psychologically traumatizing. Consumption is not recommended.
Date of Recovery: 3/4/2███
Location of Discovery: The nave of the █████████ Baptist Church, after rioting churchgoers and clergy caused the building’s partial destruction.
Current Status: All testing currently suspended following Incident 1412-A.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All it is is an apple tree that experiences the four seasons in reverse! How could that be an SCP? SCPs usually have more anomalous properties/effects than that, and there's usually some sort of story that goes with them!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I don’t know. It has potential, but I kind of feel like I’m it should be a mainlist SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This item has potential, but it's done all wrong. If I were to write it:

Item Description: A 1m by .5m mirror collection of mirror fragments of random shape that will inflict harm done to itself onto the individual who has tried to inflict the damage in the corresponding area. This effect only manifests when the person is in direct contact with the object used to harm it.
Date of Recovery: 1/7/2014
Location of discovery: Site-19 North Wing sublevel 1 Male restroom
Current Status: Destroyed during testing with firearms on █/█/██. Testing with fragments shows the same properties.
Note: Subject AI-12██ did not expire despite shooting his reelection in the forehead. Changes in knowledge have been added to the item's file.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OK. But otherwise, is it good enough for the log once it is reopened or the Log of Anomalous Items II comes out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you mean orchard, not:

Apple picking place

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Entry:

Object description: A mirror that, when punched, causes the person who punched it to sustain all injuries and pain as if they were punched.
Date of recovery: 1/7/2014
location of recovery: site 19 male restrooms
current status:destroyed after testing wether injuries by gun count.

item.title: Re: New Anomalous Item Idea
item.content:encoded: Isn't the note kind of lolFoundation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an entry I have in mind:

Item Description: An apple tree that experiences the four seasons in reverse. The leaves are all green and the fruit is ripe in the winter, the leaves and fruit shrink and disappear in the spring, the tree is bare in the summer, and leaves rise up from the ground and turn green in the fall. Apples also rise up in the fall and become ripe, and they become non-ripe in the spring.
Date of Recovery: 2/1/2018.
Location of Recovery: █████ ███████ █████, an apple-picking place in ████████, ██.
Current Status: Remains in ████████, ██. Seeds have been extracted and are currently growing at Site-19. Anomaly covered up as something done by the employees at █████ ███████ █████.

item.title: Re: Saturn
item.content:encoded: Europa is a moon of Jupiter.

item.title: Saturn
item.content:encoded: I like it. But Jupiter wouldn't work, it's a gas giant.

item.title: Re: addition?
item.content:encoded: Ok, that answers the question I was gonna post. I thought that the list was done, closed because of something.

item.title: Re: addition?
item.content:encoded:
Only after the current pruning cycle has completed. There has been a lot of material that accumulated un-reviewed in the past year or so, and staff is wrapping up with the edits.

In the meantime, you can post the entry you have in mind here, to get a response on whether or not it's good enough to be kept during the next round of pruning.

item.title: addition?
item.content:encoded: is there any way that i can add an anomalous item?

item.title: Re: Possible change
item.content:encoded: I tried with a list of preternatural people a few years back. Didn't work out so well.

item.title: Possible change
item.content:encoded: So I know the page is being pruned, but I was thinking we should divide the page into two separate pages, one for living subject and one for nonliving subjects, due to calling a human an anomalous item not making sense with the way the foundation does things, I'd call the new page for living anomalies something like: Unusual Entities

item.title: Self-fullfilling prophecy
item.content:encoded:
Item Description: An otherwise-bland stapler with the peculiar memetic effect to convince any observers that it, (regardless of their knowledge of the Foundation, how object classification works, or even what a stapler normally does) is anomalous in some way, and deserving of not only reclassification as a full SCP, but also of a "Keter" or "Apollyon" classification. Convincing effect lasts until observation is broken, though the terms "SCP", "Keter", "Apollyon", etc. are not erased.
Date of Recovery: 7/13/2011
Location of Recovery: █████ Elementary School, ████
Current Status: standard storage locker at Site-██
Notes: If you don't look at it, you're not effected, so my opinion counts! In all seriousness, it's essentially a self-fulfilling prophecy and, if not Keter as it wants, it should at least be upgraded to Safe, and warrant at least some anti-meme efforts, right? -Dr. Briggs

I don't know, some kind of self-fullfilling prophecy, like the opposite of some other SCPs/AOs that convince you they're not anomalous whatsoever, would be ironic in some capacity.

item.title: Nice Shot
item.content:encoded: Its gotta be the TF2 Scout.

item.title: Re: Re: Conversion of a failed -J item
item.content:encoded: I just posted about it in the complaints thread, and saw this immediately after. Whoops.

item.title: Re: Re: Conversion of a failed -J item
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I'm not sure what happened there. I'll nudge someone about this, see if we can maybe even get some recruits to help prune it. Might even be time for a Part II given the length.

item.title: Re: Re: Conversion of a failed -J item
item.content:encoded: I… legitimately can't argue with that. Staff has been saying that they're going to prune this for almost six months, and they've not even touched it.

item.title: Re: Re: Conversion of a failed -J item
item.content:encoded: I'm really not sure what to make of Anomalous Items, there's still some really stanky garbage in there that makes this seem passable to me by comparison.

item.title: Re: Conversion of a failed -J item
item.content:encoded: If it was a bad idea as an SCP, let alone a joke entry, what makes you think it would be any better here? This isn't a graveyard for failed skip ideas.

item.title: Conversion of a failed -J item
item.content:encoded:
So, inspired by the… interesting debacle around the article regarding the "Astolfo beanie plush" meme in the joke section I thought maybe a different take on it could work here. Posting it here to gauge reception for whenever the new page is up.

Item Description: P█████-brand "plush" toy modeled after the fictional character A████ 's depiction in the popular series ██e. Object induces a sense of paranoia regarding sexual orientation in adult male subjects who have previously affirmed their heterosexuality. Any non-masculine identifying subjects appear unaffected by the object.
Date of Recovery: ██-██-████
Location of Recovery: █████, ██████
Current Status: Contained in a sealed jar within a locker at Site-██.

item.title: New Anomalous Item Idea
item.content:encoded:
Item Description: An unbranded toothbrush that causes teeth to decline in cleanliness and become yellow.
Date of Recovery: ██-██-████
Location of Recovery: ██████, Britain
Current Status: Contained in a low-risk anomalous item Storage Locker at Site-██.
Notes: "Whoever keeps putting this in light containment's bathroom will be reprimanded heavily!" - Senior Researcher Sleezer

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice Good Omens reference, but I think the agent name has the wrong number of blackboxes.
